vehicle_profile.name: 2013 Chevrolet Impala LFX

ecu.fuel_type: E10 regular (87 octane), E10 premium (93 recommended)

known_issues: Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection, no fuel wash)
Timing chain tensioner wear (listen for rattle on cold start)
Oil consumption if not maintained (check PCV valve)
High pressure fuel pump (HPFP) can fail, $800+ part
tuning_limitations: High compression (12:1) limits boost without forged internals
Direct injection fuel system has hard limits on pump capacity
Stock pistons are cast, not forged - detonation risk
Factory timing already aggressive on 87 octane

lfx_specific_pids.name: HPFP_Pressure
lfx_specific_pids.notes: Direct injection runs very high pressure. Low pressure = weak spray pattern, misfires

lfx_specific_pids.name: LPFP_Pressure
lfx_specific_pids.description: Low Pressure Fuel Pump (tank pump) pressure (psi)
lfx_specific_pids.notes: Low pressure strains HPFP, can cause fuel starvation at high load

lfx_specific_pids.name: VVT_Intake_Actual
lfx_specific_pids.notes: Compare to commanded. Large deviation = VVT solenoid or chain issue

lfx_specific_pids.name: VVT_Exhaust_Actual
lfx_specific_pids.notes: Exhaust cam typically retarded (negative values) at idle

lfx_specific_pids.name: Fuel_Injector_Duty
lfx_specific_pids.notes: Above 85% at WOT = injectors maxed out. Need larger injectors or reduce power

lfx_specific_pids.name: Cylinder_Head_Temp
lfx_specific_pids.notes: High compression runs hot. Watch for head temp vs coolant temp delta

lfx_specific_pids.name: Fuel_Trim_Cell
lfx_specific_pids.notes: GM uses fuel trim cells. Know which cell you're in for accurate tuning

lfx_specific_pids.name: Knock_Retard_Cyl1
lfx_specific_pids.notes: LFX prone to knock on 87 octane. Monitor all 6 cylinders

spark_changes.87_octane: Stock timing, maybe +1-2 if no knock
spark_changes.warning: 12:1 compression is very sensitive to knock. Use 93 octane for any timing advance

vvt_changes.intake: +5 degrees at WOT for more power
vvt_changes.exhaust: Reduce overlap slightly for better idle quality
vvt_changes.note: Small VVT changes have big effect on torque curve

trans_changes.warning: 6T70 torque limit is ~350 lb-ft. Don't exceed with tune

special_considerations: Use 93 octane minimum for any timing advance
Monitor fuel trims in cell 14-16 (light cruise) - these are leanest
Log HPFP pressure at WOT - must maintain 12 MPa minimum
High compression means you will hear more 'ping' on 87 octane than V8s

critical_changes.vvt: Recalibrate entire VVT tables - new cam profile
critical_changes.fuel: Check injector duty - may need injectors
critical_changes.spark: Retard timing at low RPM (overlap causes reversion)

warnings: LFX cams are expensive ($1000+ for good ones)
More overlap hurts fuel economy and idle quality
May fail emissions due to overlap
Consider if worth vs saving for V8 swap

reasons: 12:1 compression too high for boost without forged pistons
Stock cast pistons will fail with detonation
Direct injection fuel system near limit stock
Crankshaft not as robust as LS/LT V8s

if_pursued.compression: Must lower to 9:1 or less (engine build)

changes.vvt: Advance intake more at cruise for better MPG
changes.fuel: Lean out light cruise cells (watch LTFT)
changes.spark: Advance at cruise if no knock (87 octane)
changes.shift: Lower shift points 200 RPM for comfort
